title: Japan’s World Cup Squad Unveiled by Head Coach; Mitoma Misses Out Due to Injury (UPDATE 1) - The Japan News
original_text:
Japan’s World Cup Squad Unveiled by Head Coach; Mitoma Misses Out Due to Injury (UPDATE 1)
The Yomiuri Shimbun
Samurai Blue head coach Hajime Moriyasu speaks at a press conference in Tokyo on Friday.
14:12 JST, May 15, 2026
The Yomiuri Shimbun
The 26-member Japan squad for the FIFA World Cup scheduled to start next month was unveiled by national team head coach Hajime Moriyasu on Friday.
Among the notable inclusions, Yuto Nagatomo of FC Tokyo, who turns 40 in September, has been selected for a fifth consecutive World Cup. He is the first Japanese player to achieve the feat. Despite suffering a right hamstring injury in mid-March, Nagatomo returned to action in early May and secured a spot on the Samurai Blue roster. Wataru Endo of Liverpool, who underwent surgery after suffering an injury to his left leg on Feb. 11, has been also called up.
High-profile players Kaoru Mitoma of Brighton and Takumi Minamino of Monaco have been left out of the tournament squad. Mitoma was substituted due to an injury during an English Premier League match against Wolverhampton on May 9. Minamino tore the anterior cruciate ligament in his left knee in December.
“I thought about the members right up until the very last moment, and I finally made up my mind at around 11 a.m. [on Friday],” Moriyasu said at a press conference in Tokyo. Regarding Mitoma, he hinted that it had been a difficult decision to not select him, saying, “I’m aware he is a major figure.”
But Moriyasu added: “Whoever plays, we will win. We will win through the team’s collective strength.”
Samurai Blue will make their eighth consecutive appearance in the tournament, which is being jointly hosted by Canada, Mexico and the United States. The team is in Group F alongside the Netherlands, Sweden and Tunisia. Japan’s opener against the Netherlands will kick off on June 15 at 5 a.m. Japan time.
